name: Ministry of Defense, Healthcare Services
description: Lead: Dr. Ehsan Hoque (Professor, University of Rochester; MIT PhD), with Sabri Boughorbel (QCRI) and Abdulrhman Aljouie (KSAU-HS).

positions.description: Research, build, and deploy clinical-grade AI for healthcare and autism (ASD) speech & language therapy. Two papers (Interspeech 2026 + preprint) emerged from this work.
projects: Architected Digital Harf, a multimodal AI therapy platform (3 therapy modules + an AI Caregiver Assistant) powered by an Agentic Synthetic Data Engine that auto-generates culturally grounded content.
Built Harf-Speech, fine-tuning Whisper, Omni-ASR, and wav2vec2 for Arabic phoneme-level assessment with advantages over proprietary Azure Cognitive Services.

Integrated into Sehhatek, Saudi Arabia's national healthcare app serving millions of users (in staging, clinical trial beginning), with partners King Fahad Armed Forces Hospital, Mohammed Bin Salman Autism Program & Madinah Autism Society.
Contributed to cross-team efforts: Waee (autism screening, University of Cambridge collaboration) and AIDA (autism screening, MIT Media Lab collaboration).

Taught ICT, introductory programming and CS basics to 7,000+ students over three years at ROOTs Edu, one of Bangladesh's leading online EdTech platforms, covering languages, data structures, algorithms, and OOP fundamentals.
Developed structured lesson plans and assessments from scratch, translating abstract CS concepts into accessible, beginner-friendly modules.
Trained and evaluated the responses, code generation, completion and debugging capabilities of popular LLMs like GPT-4 and Gemini to improve their coding capabilities.
Joined as a coding expert on the 'Dolphin Coding' project, solving programming problems and providing solutions with proper documentation and explanations that were later fed into the LLMs.
